purpose: Remove ONE document-level (WPM) sign-off from a workpaper or KC form. Supports the rule that a stale sign-off is cleared on a form after it is modified. Applying sign-offs stays human-only (add endpoints documented here but NOT scripted).
transport: XMLHttpRequest (WPM is cross-origin from engagement.cchaxcess.com; fetch fails CORS preflight). cap:wpm or ls:wpm auth.

header_case_notes.IDToken: ALL-CAPS on WPM (distinct from KC's IdToken)

body_template.engagementId: {clientId}  // MISNAMED — value is the Axcess clientId, not the engagementId
body_template.objectId: {documentId of the workpaper — GUID for KC forms, NUMERIC STRING for uploaded workpapers}
body_template.objectTypeId: {4 = KC form, 1 = uploaded workpaper (xlsx/pdf/doc). Echo the row's own value; do NOT hardcode}

response: 200 with {"signoffDetails":[...remaining sign-offs...],"documentStatus":<int>}. signoffDetails reflects the POST-removal state (empty when the last sign-off was pulled).
verify: GET /v1/Documents/{clientId}/{documentId} — the removed leg's preparedBy*/reviewedBy* fields (FirstName/LastName null, UserId all-zero, CreatedDateTime null). Or folder_get and read the row's signOffs[] array.
gotchas: Removal is keyed by (objectId, signatureType) with NO userId — the API removes whichever sign-off holds that leg, including another user's. OUT OF BOUNDS (api-ui-parity): the binder UI only lets you remove your OWN sign-off, so you must not remove anyone else's via this API. Confirm the leg's userId == the logged-in user (document_get) before calling; do NOT probe the foreign-removal path — exceeding the UI corrupts untested state.
NOT a hard delete — POST that clears a sign-off state slot, not a document DELETE; in-bounds under the wpm.py hard-delete policy.
objectTypeId is document-type-specific: 4 (KC form, GUID objectId) vs 1 (uploaded workpaper, numeric-string objectId). Read it off the row.
engagementId in the body carries the clientId (misnamed-engagementId quirk, architecture.md).
ADD endpoints (NOT scripted — human-only): POST /v1/signoff/preparer and POST /v1/signoff/reviewer take the identical body and differ only by URL + signatureType.
